sprint: Phase A.4'-C — GeoVac wedge application of the Wick-rotation bridge functor
sprint_position: Phase A.4'-C of Sprint L3e-P3 (parallel with A.4'-A G-B2 and A.4'-B G-B4)
verdict_summary: Bridge applies cleanly to Paper 43 hemispheric wedge; six newly accessible theorems identified (3 unconditional, 4 conditional on G-B2/G-B4 in A.4'-A/B); merged Paper 48 §6/§7 substrate established at outline rigor; no new structural obstruction surfaces beyond the already-named G-B2/G-B4 gaps; recommended PROCEED to A.5' synthesis after A.4'-A/B closure.

wedge_identification.wedge_definition: P_{W_L} := P_W^spatial ⊗ P_{t >= 0} per Paper 43 Def def:wedge_L

krein_ppqms_4tuple.A_K: Lorentzian operator system restricted to wedge (Paper 44 substrate)
krein_ppqms_4tuple.M_L: M-diagonal Abelian topography restricted to wedge (Phase A.2' Lemma 2.15)

wedge_identification.axiom_verification: all five Phase A.2' Def 2.16 axioms verified by inheritance from Papers 42/43/44/45 — no new verification work required

T1_geovac_wedge_as_MS_PPQMS.statement: For every finite cutoff (n_max, N_t, T) with T = 2π canonical, the GeoVac hemispheric wedge W_L defines a Mondino-Sämann covered Lorentzian pre-length space (𝓜̂^L|_{W_L}, ℓ^L_wedge, ω̂_W^L, Û^L_wedge) ∈ LorPLG_cov with timelike diameter 2π uniform across all cover levels.
T1_geovac_wedge_as_MS_PPQMS.substantive_content: Existence statement of synthetic LPLS from the GeoVac wedge by direct construction

T2_synthetic_compactness_geovac_wedge_fa.statement: Any sequence of GeoVac wedge Krein PPQMS at admissible-scaling cutoffs has a subsequence whose bridge images converge in Mondino-Sämann pLGH sense to a covered LPLS limit.
T2_synthetic_compactness_geovac_wedge_fa.mechanism: A.3' Theorem 6.4(B3) gives three MS Thm 6.2 pre-compactness conditions at theorem-grade rigor; the mechanical MS Def 3.6 axiom verification is G-B4.

T3_synthetic_bit_exact_panel_inheritance.statement: The Mondino-Sämann pLGH convergence rate of the GeoVac wedge sequence at Paper 45 panel cells satisfies d_pLGH^wedge ≤ {2.0746, 1.6101, 1.3223} at (n_max, N_t) ∈ {(2,3), (3,5), (4,7)}, bit-identical to the Paper 45 Krein-side Λ^L values.
T3_synthetic_bit_exact_panel_inheritance.substantive_content: FIRST quantitative pLGH-convergence panel in the Mondino-Sämann literature derived from operator-algebraic input. All extant MS-style examples (arXiv:2504.10380 v4 §10) are constructed geometrically from continuous spacetimes; no operator-algebraic derivations exist.

T4_four_witness_synthetic_readings.statement: The Paper 42 four-witness theorem identifies four thermal-time structures (BW, HH, Sewell, Unruh) on the operator-algebraic side; under the bridge, these give four geometric-time readings on the synthetic side, with witness pairs of equal κ_g producing IDENTICAL Lorentzian pre-length spaces.
T4_four_witness_synthetic_readings.mechanism: ℓ^L = κ_g · τ_mod rescales linearly with κ_g; bit-exact algebra-action collapse of four witnesses (Paper 42 §8 Cor 8.1) translates to bit-exact metric-rescaling identification on synthetic side

T5_synthetic_pythagorean_orthogonality_M.statement: Under the bridge W applied to the GeoVac wedge at the Riemannian limit N_t = 1, H_local and D_W^L correspond to two structurally orthogonal foliation structures on the synthetic wedge pre-length space: boost-orbit foliation F_α (Π_W-even) and spinor-bundle foliation F_D (Π_W-odd). The Paper 43 Pythagorean closed-form residual translates to a synthetic-side decomposition of wedge geometric data into κ_g²-scaling Hopf-base-measure contribution (S(n_max)/(4π²)) and κ_g-independent spinor-bundle contribution (D(n_max)).
T5_synthetic_pythagorean_orthogonality_M.M1_signature: 1/π² = Vol(S¹)^{-2} = inverse-squared bridge cover slab-period (timelike diameter 2π, M1 mechanism preserved functorially)
T5_synthetic_pythagorean_orthogonality_M.substantive_content: M1 content is bit-exactly transported across the bridge; the master Mellin engine M1 mechanism is functorially preserved by W under κ_g-rescaling

T6_G2_metric_level_closure_geovac_wedge.statement: For the GeoVac hemispheric wedge specifically, the de-compactification limit T → ∞ closes at the propinquity-equivalent level on the synthetic side: the synthetic wedge images at admissible-scaling cutoffs converge in Mondino-Sämann pLGH topology to the synthetic non-compact wedge object.
T6_G2_metric_level_closure_geovac_wedge.headline_significance: DEEPEST SUBSTANTIVE NEW CONTENT of the wedge application. Closes G2 (Paper 45 §1.4) at metric-level on the synthetic side for the GeoVac wedge — a workaround for the published-open non-compact propinquity problem (no published non-compact Latrémolière propinquity exists in math.OA literature as of 2026).
T6_G2_metric_level_closure_geovac_wedge.mechanism: Paper 47 §6 Cor compact_equivalent gives spectral-level equivalence of three carriers; under the bridge W, all three are well-defined MS LPLS (T1 applies to each); the norm-resolvent convergence on the Krein side plus the bridge functoriality plus G-B2 + G-B4 give pLGH convergence on the synthetic side.

T6a_synthetic_three_carrier_identificati.statement: The Paper 47 §6 three-carrier identification on the operator-algebraic side translates via the bridge W to a synthetic-side three-carrier identification: the three synthetic wedge images (W(X^K_per), W(X^K_Dir), W(X^K_R)) are isometric as Mondino-Sämann covered LPLS in the limit T → ∞.
T6a_synthetic_three_carrier_identificati.mechanism: Paper 47 isometric embeddings J̃_T^per, J̃_T^Dir : K_T^bullet ↪ K_R commute with M-diagonal topographies (time-independent by Phase A.2' Lemma 2.15); descend to maps Ĵ_T^per, Ĵ_T^Dir on synthetic side via Gelfand duality.
T6a_synthetic_three_carrier_identificati.substantive_content: Until this sprint, three-carrier identification was purely operator-algebraic (Paper 47 Thm three_carriers). The bridge reveals it also holds at synthetic-Lorentzian level — three compact-approximation schemes (Papers 43/45/47) are synthetically-Lorentzian-isometric in the limit, not just spectrally equivalent.

Paper 45 main theorem + Table tab:panel — load-bearing for §6.4

subsections: §7.1 Synthetic compactness of the GeoVac wedge family (T2, conditional G-B4)
§7.2 First quantitative pLGH-convergence panel from operator-algebraic input (T3 + structural commentary, unconditional)
§7.3 G2 metric-level closure for the GeoVac wedge via the bridge (T6, conditional G-B2 + G-B4)
§7.4 Why this is genuinely new content for Mondino-Sämann pLGH theory
§7.5 Open question Q1' (strong-form bridge with enlarged substrate)
cross_references: Mondino-Sämann arXiv:2504.10380 v4 — load-bearing throughout for synthetic-side target
Paper 45 §1.4 G1/G2 open questions — load-bearing for §7.3, §7.5
Connes-Rovelli 1994 thermal-time — load-bearing for T4 synthetic interpretation

Merged Paper 48 §6 / §7 substrate at outline rigor
No structural obstruction beyond already-named G-B2/G-B4 gaps

anticipated_failure_modes_none_surfaced.could_surface: M-diagonal topography M^L|_{W_L} is empty / degenerate at wedge restriction
anticipated_failure_modes_none_surfaced.did_not_because: topography well-defined as wedge restriction of full topography, cardinality matches dim W_L

anticipated_failure_modes_none_surfaced.could_surface: timelike diameter sup τ_mod on wedge not uniformly bounded
anticipated_failure_modes_none_surfaced.did_not_because: Paper 43 Thm bw_alpha_lorentz gives 2π-periodicity bit-exactly, hence uniform 2π bound

anticipated_failure_modes_none_surfaced.could_surface: cover doesn't naturally decompose into MS-style cover at scales β_k
anticipated_failure_modes_none_surfaced.did_not_because: canonical β_k = 2π at all k matches MS Def 3.8(iv) slab bound exactly

anticipated_failure_modes_none_surfaced.could_surface: Pythagorean orthogonality doesn't translate via bridge
anticipated_failure_modes_none_surfaced.did_not_because: Π_W-even / Π_W-odd decomposition transports because Π_W is part of wedge structure preserved by bridge

anticipated_failure_modes_none_surfaced.could_surface: three-carrier identification breaks on synthetic side
anticipated_failure_modes_none_surfaced.did_not_because: Paper 47 isometric embeddings commute with topography (time-independent), descend to synthetic-side isometric embeddings via Gelfand duality
